Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Temporarily Transfer Trust Property Into Conservatorship Estate In Order To Obtain Reverse Mortgage
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-01-281665 Conservatorship of Stephenson & PTR-14-298357 Stephenson Trust.
Appearances: Ernest Der for and with (Petitioner) Reginald Stephenson. Court appointed counsel Bruce Feder for (Conservatee) Kenneth Stephenson. Roberta Stephenson (wife) also present. Kenneth Stephenson, Jr., (son) in Propria Persona.

The Court explained to Ms. Stephenson that she is not able to represent the children. The Court stated that the petition brings up difficult questions to unravel, that the relief would benefit the brother more than the conservatee or the beneficiaries, acknowledged that Reginald is doing important work, concerns regarding the controls that would be in place, adequate protection for the contingent beneficiaries, concerns regarding an adverse reaction from Medi-Cal and that it fully understands the importance of Reginald's contributions to the care of Kenneth. Mr. Feder reported that Kenneth's share would not be reduced by the money for Reginald, suggested capping the amount available to Reginald, suggested reports be generated anytime money is withdrawn from the equity, that they would monitor the value of the property, that the repairs made to the property would increase its value, that there would be no effect to Medi-Cal as the property is an exempt asset, that there would be no amendment to the trust or new trust required, that they would only be taking the property out and changing legal title temporarily until the close of the loan and that Kenneth has been the responsible person in Kenneth's life for the last 14 years and has never asked for fees. Mr. Feder explained the structure of the trust and how the payback would work, that there is no intent to change the terms of the trust, that it is just a legal step that needs to be taken to obtain the loan and that it will not change the nature of the trust. Mr. Der stated that Medi-Cal recipients usually gift the property to family members or place in an irrevocable trust, that the home is considered exempt and that the property will go back into the special needs trust. Both counsel spoke of the mother's wish for both children to have this home, that she wanted it to be for their lifetime, that they should try to protect Kenneth's half but allow the loan for Reginald to continue to live there, that the repairs will increase the value and that they can provide reports in the accountings. Ms. Stephenson spoke of the current value of the house, her concerns regarding Reginald as the conservator and her concerns as to how the money will be paid back. Mr. Kenneth Stephenson, Jr. stated that he has spoken with his Uncle regarding the deterioration of the house, that his Uncle is trying to get the house up to standards, that this is not about money but about what his grandmother would want, that he is in support of the petition and what his Uncle wants to do, that neither he, his father or his sister would be losing out, that there is no conflict going on here, that his grandmother wanted the family taken care of and that he believes the legal team will do the right thing. Mr. Reginald Stephenson stated that his mother has no intent of this being drawn out, that he would like to live the rest of his days in dignity, that he has no intent to be shady and that if the loan is not approved he will still have to survive but would like to survive with dignity. The Petition for Substituted Judgment to Obtain an FHA Insured Home Equity Conversion Mortgage, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Temporarily Transfer Trust Property Into Conservatorship Estate In Order To Obtain Reverse Mortgage
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Continued To Apr-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PCN-01-281665 Conservatorship of Kenneth Stephenson and PTR-14-298357 The Kenneth Stephenson Trust.
Appearances: Deborah Fox for and with (petitioner) Reginald Stephenson. Court appointed counsel Bruce Feder for (conservatee) Kenneth Stephenson. Roberta Stephenson (wife) in Propria Persona.

The court stated that Mr. Feder had previously been discharged and it was determined that Mr. Feder was reappointed on June 24, 2013, commended Mr. R. Stephenson on carrying out his duty as conservator, expressed concerns with the risk of taking the property out of the trust and stated the property is in a trust with expressed prohibition against doing this. Ms. Fox reported that the drafting attorney is deceased, the settlor was in her 90's and ill at the time and feels the prohibition clause is a phrase that was inserted without real intention or her knowledge of what it meant, that the alternative is to sell the property and for Mr. R. Stephenson to move out of state, the risks are low and that there is capable counsel looking out for the conservatee. Mr. Feder reported that the conservatee does not get many visitors, the attention he receives from his brother is extraordinary, the reverse mortgage would be favorable to Kenneth, that there are ways to track spending and reconcile in the future and that the mother would not want this bar in the trust if it meant the house being sold. Ms. Fox reported that the property was formally appraised at $550,000.00 and that $290,000.00 would be the amount obtained by the reverse mortgage. Mr. Reginald Stephenson stated that he was present with the drafting attorney, is sure his mother did not wish to "handcuff me" in providing care for his brother and expressed that this has been extremely difficult and will be stressful if the reverse mortgage does not happen. Ms. Roberta Stephenson stated that her and her children visit Mr. Stephenson 3-4 times per month, expressed her concerns over her children's inheritance and stated they were not aware that Mr. R. Stephenson had been appointed as conservator. The court stated that it is not comfortable proceeding with what will change the trust, that the petitioners need to decide whether or not to petition for a trust amendment, to supplement the existing petitions to address concerns raised by the court and the examiners, does not want the public benefits jeopardized and to explain how the funds will be used as well as how the conservatee's heirs would be protected. The petition for substituted judgment, etc., (PCN-01-281665) and the petition for order instructing trustee, etc., (PTR-14-298357) were continued to April 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court authorizes that a petition to amend the trust can be set for the same date as long as it is filed in time for proper notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)
